title: hieracium prenanthoides subsp. bupleurifolioides
intro: Hieracium prenanthoides subsp. bupleurifolioides, also known as hawkweed, is a subspecies of the Hieracium prenanthoides plant, which is a type of flowering plant in the dandelion family. It is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic properties, making it beneficial for treating various health conditions. The health benefits of Hieracium prenanthoides subsp. bupleurifolioides include reducing inflammation, alleviating digestive issues, and relieving pain. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and it can also be used topically as a poultice or ointment to promote healing and reduce inflammation.
